fsm_model.section_name: 10.6 Information Blocks - Message Processing State Machine

states.name: ActionIdle
states.description: No active interaction model Action in progress, awaiting new message or Action start

states.name: ActionStarted_NoCompression
states.description: Interaction model Action started, currently processing paths without tag compression active

states.name: CompressionTracking
states.description: Tag compression base established, tracking path components for subsequent compressed paths
invariants: tracked_node != null || tracked_endpoint != null || tracked_cluster != null || tracked_attribute != null

states.name: CompressionActive
states.description: Processing compressed path, inheriting components from tracked base path

states.name: PathExpansionRequired
states.description: Path contains wildcards and must be expanded to concrete paths before processing

states.name: PathExpanded
states.description: Wildcard path expanded into list of concrete paths ready for processing

states.name: ListOperationIdle
states.description: No list operation in progress, ready to process new list mutation

states.name: ListSingleIB_Processing
states.description: Processing single AttributeDataIB containing full list replacement in one message
invariants: message_context == ReportDataMessage || (message_context == WriteRequestMessage && target_attribute in [ACL, Extension])

states.name: ListMultiIB_ClearSignaled
states.description: First AttributeDataIB with empty array received, list clear signaled, awaiting append IBs

states.name: ListMultiIB_PartialReplaceSignaled
states.description: First AttributeDataIB with non-empty array received, partial list provided, awaiting more append IBs

states.name: ListAppending
states.description: Processing AttributeDataIB with ListIndex=null to append item to list

states.name: ListOperationComplete
states.description: List mutation operation completed successfully, final list assembled

states.name: ListOperationError
states.description: List mutation encountered error, final list state uncertain per spec

states.name: CommandIdle
states.description: No command invocation in progress, ready to receive invoke request

states.name: CommandPending_SingleCommand
states.description: Single command invoked without CommandRef (optional), awaiting response

states.name: CommandPending_MultiCommand
states.description: Multiple commands invoked with CommandRefs (required), awaiting correlated responses

states.name: CommandResponseReceived
states.description: Response received for pending command, correlating via CommandRef if present

states.name: EventProcessingIdle
states.description: No event processing in progress, awaiting event data or subscription

states.name: EventProcessing_AbsoluteTimestamp
states.description: Processing event with absolute timestamp (EpochTimestamp or SystemTimestamp)

states.name: EventProcessing_DeltaTimestamp
states.description: Processing event with delta timestamp relative to prior event in stream

states.name: EventMonotonicityCheckFailed
states.description: Event received with EventNumber not greater than last, monotonicity violation detected

states.name: TLVValidationInProgress
states.description: Validating TLV structure encoding against schema requirements

states.name: TLVValidationPassed
states.description: TLV structure validated successfully, all required fields present, types correct

states.name: TLVValidationFailed
states.description: TLV validation failed due to missing required fields, wrong types, or ordering violations

states.name: ErrorState_InvalidPath
states.description: Path validation failed due to invalid structure or unsupported nesting

states.name: ErrorState_CompressionStateCorruption
states.description: Tag compression state corrupted or leaked across Action boundaries

states.name: ErrorState_CommandCorrelationFailure
states.description: Command response correlation failed due to missing or duplicate CommandRef

states.name: DataVersionTracking
states.description: Tracking DataVersion from uncompressed AttributeDataIB for inheritance by subsequent compressed IBs

states.name: DataVersionFilterMatching
states.description: Matching DataVersionFilterIB against current attribute DataVersion to determine if data should be included

states.name: DataVersionFilterPassed
states.description: DataVersion filter matched, data version has changed and should be sent

states.name: DataVersionFilterFailed
states.description: DataVersion filter not matched, data version unchanged and should be suppressed

states.name: EventFilterMatching
states.description: Matching EventFilterIB against event to determine if event meets EventMin threshold

states.name: EventFilterPassed
states.description: Event filter passed, EventNumber >= EventMin threshold, event should be included

states.name: EventFilterFailed
states.description: Event filter failed, EventNumber < EventMin threshold, event should be suppressed

states.name: TimestampTypeValidating
states.description: Validating that exactly one timestamp type is present in EventDataIB (one-of constraint)

states.name: EpochTimestampProcessing
states.description: Processing event with Epoch-based timestamp (EpochTimestamp or DeltaEpochTimestamp)

states.name: SystemTimestampProcessing
states.description: Processing event with System-based timestamp (SystemTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp)

states.name: InvokeResponseValidating
states.description: Validating InvokeResponseIB has exactly one of Command or Status (mutual exclusion)

states.name: InvokeResponseValid
states.description: InvokeResponseIB validated, exactly one of Command or Status present
invariants: (has_command == true && has_status == false) || (has_command == false && has_status == true)

states.name: ErrorState_InvokeResponseInvalid
states.description: InvokeResponseIB validation failed, both or neither of Command and Status present
invariants: (has_command == true && has_status == true) || (has_command == false && has_status == false)

states.name: ErrorState_TimestampValidationFailed
states.description: Timestamp validation failed, multiple or no timestamp types present in EventDataIB

actions: resolved_path := resolve_compressed_path(path, tracked_node, tracked_endpoint, tracked_cluster, tracked_attribute)

transitions.guard_condition: ib.path.listindex == omitted && is_array(ib.data) && (message_type == ReportDataMessage || (message_type == WriteRequestMessage && ib.path.attribute in [ACL, Extension]))
response.commandref == null || response.commandref not in command_refs
event.eventnumber > last_event_number && event.delta_timestamp != null && event.absolute_timestamps == null
(has_command == true && has_status == false) || (has_command == false && has_status == true)
(has_command == true && has_status == true) || (has_command == false && has_status == false)

functions.name: generate_action_id
functions.description: Generates unique identifier for new interaction model Action to track tag compression scope boundaries
functions.algorithm_detail: Monotonically increasing counter or timestamp-based ID generation ensuring uniqueness within session
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from ActionIdle to ActionStarted states to establish compression tracking scope

functions.name: extract_node

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path structure containing optional Node field

functions.description: Extracts Node field from path, returns null if omitted and matches server NodeID, returns wildcard marker if omitted and not matching
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if path.node exists: if yes return path.node, if no check if target matches server NodeID (return null), else return wildcard marker
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing compression tracking state to record Node component for inheritance

functions.name: extract_endpoint

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path structure containing optional Endpoint field

functions.description: Extracts Endpoint field from path, returns wildcard marker if omitted per EnableTagCompression rules
functions.algorithm_detail: If path.endpoint exists return path.endpoint, else if EnableTagCompression==false return wildcard, else return null for compression inheritance
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing compression tracking state to record Endpoint component

functions.name: extract_cluster

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path structure containing optional Cluster field

functions.description: Extracts Cluster field from path, returns wildcard marker if omitted per EnableTagCompression rules
functions.algorithm_detail: If path.cluster exists return path.cluster, else if EnableTagCompression==false return wildcard, else return null for compression inheritance
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing compression tracking state to record Cluster component

functions.name: extract_attribute

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path structure containing optional Attribute field

functions.description: Extracts Attribute field from path, returns wildcard marker if omitted per EnableTagCompression rules
functions.algorithm_detail: If path.attribute exists return path.attribute, else if EnableTagCompression==false return wildcard, else return null for compression inheritance
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing compression tracking state to record Attribute component

functions.name: resolve_compressed_path
functions.description: Resolves compressed path by filling omitted fields with tracked values from last uncompressed path in Action
functions.algorithm_detail: For each field in compressed_path: if field is present use its value, else use corresponding tracked value. Preserve wildcard semantics if tracked value is wildcard. Return fully resolved PathIB structure.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to CompressionActive to reconstruct full path from compressed representation

functions.name: has_wildcard_components

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to check for wildcard components

functions.description: Checks if path contains any wildcard components (omitted Endpoint, Cluster, Attribute, Event when EnableTagCompression is false)
functions.algorithm_detail: Return true if any of (Endpoint, Cluster, Attribute, Event) is omitted and EnableTagCompression is false or absent, else return false
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to determine if path expansion is required before processing

functions.name: is_wildcard
functions.description: Returns true if component is null or explicitly marked as wildcard
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if component is null or has wildcard marker value, return boolean result
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when detecting which path components need expansion

functions.name: expand_wildcards
functions.description: Expands wildcard path into list of all concrete paths matching the wildcard pattern by querying node structure
functions.algorithm_detail: For each wildcard component, enumerate all valid values from node's endpoint/cluster/attribute structure. Generate Cartesian product of all valid combinations. Return list of concrete PathIB structures.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from PathExpansionRequired to PathExpanded to generate concrete paths

functions.name: is_array
functions.description: Checks if data is encoded as TLV array type
functions.algorithm_detail: Inspect TLV type tag, return true if array type, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when determining list operation pattern (single-IB vs multi-IB)

functions.name: is_empty_array
functions.description: Checks if data is an empty TLV array signaling list clear operation
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if data is array type AND array length is zero, return boolean
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to detect list clear signal in multi-IB pattern

functions.name: is_non_empty_array
functions.description: Checks if data is non-empty TLV array
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if data is array type AND array length > 0, return boolean
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to detect partial list replacement signal in multi-IB pattern

functions.name: validate_list_schema
functions.description: Validates that list items conform to schema defined for attribute in cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieve schema for cluster.attribute, iterate through list_data items checking each against schema (field types, required fields, value ranges). Return true if all items valid, false if any invalid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating list replacement or append operations

functions.name: apply_list_replacement
functions.description: Atomically replaces entire list at target path with new list contents
functions.algorithm_detail: Lock attribute, clear existing list, write new_list items, update DataVersion, unlock attribute. Operation is atomic at attribute level per spec.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when list operation completes successfully to commit changes

functions.name: append_item
functions.description: Appends item to accumulator list during multi-IB list assembly
functions.algorithm_detail: Add item to end of list array
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing ListIndex=null append operations

functions.name: validate_list_item
functions.description: Validates single list item against schema
functions.algorithm_detail: Check item structure against list element schema, return true if valid
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when appending items to validate each before accumulation

functions.name: check_partial_application
functions.description: Checks if some list items were applied before error occurred
functions.algorithm_detail: Inspect list update state, return true if any items were written before error, false if no changes applied
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in error scenarios to determine list state certainty per spec requirement

functions.name: create_status_ib
functions.description: Creates StatusIB structure with specified status code
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct TLV structure with Status field set to status_code, optionally add ClusterStatus if cluster-specific error
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating error responses

functions.name: all_have_commandref
functions.description: Checks if all commands in list have CommandRef field present
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through commands, return false if any command.commandref is null, return true if all have values
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating multi-command invoke requests

functions.name: commandrefs_unique
functions.description: Checks if all CommandRef values in command list are unique
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract all commandref values into set, compare set size to list size. If equal, all unique, return true. If set smaller, duplicates exist, return false.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating multi-command invoke requests for correlation integrity

functions.name: extract_commandrefs
functions.description: Extracts all CommandRef values from command list into set
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through commands, collect command.commandref values into set, return set
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing command correlation tracking state

functions.name: build_ref_map
functions.description: Builds map from CommandRef to corresponding CommandDataIB for response correlation
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through commands, create map entry commandref -> command, return map
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing multi-command correlation tracking

functions.name: extract_commandref
functions.description: Extracts CommandRef from response (Command or Status field)
functions.algorithm_detail: If response has Command field return command.commandref, if has Status field return status.commandref
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when correlating responses to pending commands

functions.name: extract_commandref_or_null
functions.description: Extracts CommandRef from response, returns null if absent
functions.algorithm_detail: Try to extract commandref from response.command or response.status, return null if field absent
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in error scenarios when commandref may be missing

functions.name: remove_from_pending
functions.description: Removes matched command from pending list after receiving response
functions.algorithm_detail: Remove command with matching commandref from pending_commands list and command_refs set
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning from CommandResponseReceived back to pending state

functions.name: process_command_response
functions.description: Processes command response by invoking appropriate handler for command result or error status
functions.algorithm_detail: If response contains Command field, extract response data and pass to command handler. If contains Status field, process error status. Update command state accordingly.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when finalizing command invocation after correlation

functions.name: extract_timestamp
functions.description: Extracts absolute timestamp (EpochTimestamp or SystemTimestamp) from event
functions.algorithm_detail: Check which timestamp field is present (EpochTimestamp or SystemTimestamp), return its value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing events with absolute timestamps

functions.name: extract_delta_type
functions.description: Determines type of delta timestamp (DeltaEpochTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp)
functions.algorithm_detail: Check which delta timestamp field is present, return enum indicating type
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to delta timestamp processing mode

functions.name: extract_delta_value
functions.description: Extracts delta timestamp value from event
functions.algorithm_detail: Read DeltaEpochTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp field value and return
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing events with delta timestamps to reconstruct absolute time

functions.name: process_event
functions.description: Processes event data by invoking appropriate event handler for cluster and event ID
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract event path, lookup handler for cluster.event, invoke handler with event.data payload
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when event passes all validation and is ready for consumption

functions.name: process_event_with_reconstructed_timestamp
functions.description: Processes event with timestamp reconstructed from delta encoding
functions.algorithm_detail: Attach reconstructed timestamp to event, then invoke event handler
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing delta-encoded events after timestamp reconstruction

functions.name: validate_all_fields
functions.description: Validates all fields in structure against schema: required fields present, types correct, ranges valid
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through schema.required_fields checking presence in structure. For each field, validate type matches schema.field_type and value in schema.field_range. Return success if all checks pass, failure with error list otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during TLV validation to ensure structure conformance

functions.name: validate_tag_ordering
functions.description: Validates that context tags in structure are emitted in order per spec requirement
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract all context tag numbers from structure in order encountered. Check if list is monotonically increasing. Return true if ordered, false if any tag is out of order.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during TLV validation to enforce tag ordering requirement

functions.name: collect_validation_errors
functions.description: Collects all validation errors encountered during structure validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Gather all errors from validation_errors accumulator, format as list of error descriptions
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to TLVValidationFailed state

functions.name: categorize_errors
functions.description: Categorizes validation errors by type (missing_field, wrong_type, wrong_order, invalid_value)
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through errors, classify each into category based on error description, return list of categories
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to populate error_types in validation failure state

functions.name: get_schema
functions.description: Retrieves schema definition for given structure type from specification
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup structure_type in schema registry, return corresponding schema definition with required fields, types, and constraints
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when beginning validation to obtain expected schema

functions.name: get_path_schema
functions.description: Retrieves schema for specific path type
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup path_type schema with field definitions, return schema
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating path structures

functions.name: generate_error_status
functions.description: Generates error status response and sends to requester
functions.algorithm_detail: Create StatusIB with status_code, encapsulate in appropriate response message type, send response
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when errors detected to report back to sender

functions.name: generate_event
functions.description: Generates internal event for state machine observation or logging
functions.algorithm_detail: Create event record with event_name and current context, add to event log or notify observers
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to signal completion or errors in list operations

functions.name: process_resolved_path
functions.description: Processes fully resolved path for read/write/subscribe operation
functions.algorithm_detail: Perform operation specified by path context (read attribute, write attribute, subscribe to events, invoke command) using resolved path components
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after path resolution completes

functions.name: create_error_message
functions.description: Formats error text into standard error message
functions.algorithm_detail: Wrap error_text with context information (state, path, action), return formatted string
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating error messages for invalid paths

functions.name: clear_list
functions.description: Returns empty list
functions.algorithm_detail: Create and return new empty list structure
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when resetting list accumulators

functions.name: clear_set
functions.description: Returns empty set
functions.algorithm_detail: Create and return new empty set structure
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when resetting command reference tracking

functions.name: clear_map
functions.description: Returns empty map
functions.algorithm_detail: Create and return new empty map structure
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when resetting command correlation map

functions.name: copy_array
functions.description: Creates deep copy of array
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through array elements, copy each to new array, return new array
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when storing initial items in partial list replacement

functions.name: array_size
functions.description: Returns number of elements in array
functions.algorithm_detail: Return array.length property
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when determining if array is partial or complete

functions.name: extract_dataversion
functions.description: Extracts DataVersion field from AttributeDataIB
functions.algorithm_detail: Return attribute_data_ib.dataversion field value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing DataVersion compression tracking

functions.name: get_matching_filter
functions.description: Finds DataVersionFilterIB matching the given path
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through filters, compare filter.path with given path checking Node/Endpoint/Cluster equality, return first match or null
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when checking if current attribute has associated filter

functions.name: get_current_dataversion
functions.description: Retrieves current DataVersion for the cluster at given path
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup cluster dataversion from internal state tracking, return current value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when comparing against filter DataVersion

functions.name: get_matching_event_filter
functions.description: Finds EventFilterIB matching the given event path
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through filters, compare filter.node with event path node if present, return first match or null
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when checking if current event has associated filter

functions.name: extract_timestamp_fields
functions.description: Extracts which timestamp fields are present in EventDataIB
functions.algorithm_detail: Check for presence of EpochTimestamp, SystemTimestamp, DeltaEpochTimestamp, DeltaSystemTimestamp fields, return list of field names that are present
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating one-of timestamp constraint

functions.name: is_epoch_timestamp
functions.description: Checks if timestamp type is Epoch-based (EpochTimestamp or DeltaEpochTimestamp)
functions.algorithm_detail: Return true if timestamp_type is EpochTimestamp or DeltaEpochTimestamp, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when determining which timestamp processing state to transition to

functions.name: is_system_timestamp
functions.description: Checks if timestamp type is System-based (SystemTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp)
functions.algorithm_detail: Return true if timestamp_type is SystemTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when determining which timestamp processing state to transition to

functions.name: extract_epoch_timestamp_value
functions.description: Extracts Epoch timestamp value from event (absolute or delta)
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if EpochTimestamp field present return its value, else if DeltaEpochTimestamp present return its value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing Epoch-based timestamp events

functions.name: extract_system_timestamp_value
functions.description: Extracts System timestamp value from event (absolute or delta)
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if SystemTimestamp field present return its value, else if DeltaSystemTimestamp present return its value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing System-based timestamp events

functions.name: has_absolute_timestamp
functions.description: Checks if event has absolute timestamp (EpochTimestamp or SystemTimestamp)
functions.algorithm_detail: Return true if EpochTimestamp or SystemTimestamp field is present, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when determining event processing path after filter

functions.name: prepare_attribute_report
functions.description: Prepares attribute for inclusion in report message
functions.algorithm_detail: Add attribute to report message being constructed, update message size tracking
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when filter passes and data should be included

functions.name: skip_attribute_report
functions.description: Skips current attribute in report due to filter suppression
functions.algorithm_detail: Move to next attribute in iteration, do not add to report message
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when filter fails and data should be suppressed

functions.name: prepare_event_report
functions.description: Prepares event for inclusion in report message
functions.algorithm_detail: Add event to report message being constructed, update message size tracking
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when event filter passes

functions.name: skip_event_report
functions.description: Skips current event in report due to filter suppression
functions.algorithm_detail: Move to next event in iteration, do not add to report message
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when event filter fails

functions.name: process_event_with_epoch_timestamp
functions.description: Processes event with Epoch timestamp, delivering to event consumers
functions.algorithm_detail: Validate event against cluster schema, check monotonicity if applicable, deliver to subscribed clients with Epoch timestamp
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when completing Epoch timestamp event processing

functions.name: process_event_with_system_timestamp
functions.description: Processes event with System timestamp, delivering to event consumers
functions.algorithm_detail: Validate event against cluster schema, check monotonicity if applicable, deliver to subscribed clients with System timestamp
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when completing System timestamp event processing

functions.name: process_validated_response
functions.description: Processes validated InvokeResponseIB based on response type
functions.algorithm_detail: If response_type is command, process command response. If status, process status response. Update correlation state.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after successful mutual exclusion validation

definitions.definition: A complete interaction model operation (Read, Write, Subscribe, Invoke) encompassing one or more message exchanges. Tag compression state is scoped to a single Action and SHALL NOT leak across Action boundaries.
definitions.security_relevance: Action boundary isolation prevents compression state from one operation affecting another, preventing path confusion attacks

definitions.definition: Mechanism where EnableTagCompression=true causes omitted path components to inherit values from last uncompressed path within same Action, reducing message size. Wildcard semantics still apply to inherited omissions.
definitions.security_relevance: Compression state management errors can cause operations to target wrong paths leading to unauthorized access

definitions.definition: Path with omitted Endpoint, Cluster, Attribute, or Event components (when EnableTagCompression is false) indicating selection of all matching entities. Requires expansion before authorization checks.
definitions.security_relevance: Wildcard expansion before ACL checks prevents coarse-grained authorization from granting access to individually unauthorized resources

definitions.definition: Three patterns for list mutation: (1) Single-IB full replacement, (2) Multi-IB empty+appends, (3) Multi-IB partial+appends. ACL and Extension attributes allow single-IB in WriteRequest, others do not.
definitions.security_relevance: Different patterns have different atomicity guarantees. Errors leave uncertain state per spec, requiring clients to re-read lists

definitions.definition: ListIndex omitted indicates list-level operation (REPLACE). ListIndex=null indicates append operation (ADD). Any other value is error. Null only valid in AttributeDataIB for write operations.
definitions.security_relevance: Numeric ListIndex values are forbidden and SHALL be rejected to prevent undefined behavior and potential out-of-bounds access

definitions.definition: Unsigned 16-bit integer correlating command requests to responses. Required for multi-command InvokeRequests, optional for single-command. Values MUST be unique within request.
definitions.security_relevance: Proper correlation prevents response confusion where one command's result is applied to different command's context

definitions.definition: EventNumber SHALL monotonically increase within an event stream, enabling replay detection and ordering. Non-monotonic EventNumbers indicate replay or reordering attack.
definitions.security_relevance: Monotonicity checking detects replayed events that could trigger stale state transitions

definitions.definition: Events can use DeltaEpochTimestamp or DeltaSystemTimestamp relative to prior event instead of absolute timestamp. When delta present, all other timestamp fields SHALL be omitted. Same units as base timestamp.
definitions.security_relevance: Delta reconstruction requires tracking last absolute timestamp. Errors in reconstruction break temporal ordering for time-based security checks

definitions.definition: All context tags SHALL be emitted in order as defined in specification to reduce receiver parsing complexity. Out-of-order tags violate protocol.
definitions.security_relevance: Strict ordering simplifies parsers and prevents exploitation of ambiguous tag sequences

definitions.definition: AttributeReportIB contains either AttributeStatus XOR AttributeData. EventReportIB contains either EventStatus XOR EventData. InvokeResponseIB contains either Command XOR Status. Never both, never neither.
definitions.security_relevance: Unambiguous success/error indication prevents confusion attacks where both or neither present

definitions.definition: Node tag MAY be omitted if target node matches server NodeID. When omitted, implicitly refers to local node, not wildcard (except for wildcards in other components).
definitions.security_relevance: Incorrect Node omission interpretation causes routing to wrong node breaking isolation

definitions.definition: When Group field present in IM representation, Group ID SHALL be encoded in DST header field and elided from path encoding to avoid redundancy.
definitions.security_relevance: Split encoding between header and payload requires consistent validation to prevent group confusion attacks

definitions.definition: All structures SHALL conform to defined schema: required fields present, correct types, values in range, tags in order. Validation failures result in CONSTRAINT_ERROR status.
definitions.security_relevance: Schema validation prevents type confusion, buffer overflows, and injection attacks via malformed structures

definitions.definition: Process of converting wildcard path into list of all concrete paths matching the pattern by enumerating node structure. Required before ACL checks.
definitions.security_relevance: Pre-expansion ACL checking ensures fine-grained authorization on each concrete path, preventing wildcard privilege escalation

definitions.definition: 32-bit unsigned integer tracking attribute version for optimistic concurrency. Can be omitted when EnableTagCompression=true, inheriting from last uncompressed AttributeDataIB.
definitions.security_relevance: Version tracking enables detection of stale updates and lost update prevention

definitions.definition: Filter structure containing ClusterPathIB and DataVersion used in Read/Subscribe requests to suppress data when version unchanged. Server compares filter DataVersion with current DataVersion, only sending data if different.
definitions.security_relevance: Correct filter matching prevents unnecessary data transmission and ensures clients receive timely updates without message bloat

definitions.definition: Filter structure containing optional Node and EventMin threshold used in event subscriptions. Events with EventNumber < EventMin are suppressed. Used to resume event streams from known point.
definitions.security_relevance: Correct EventMin filtering prevents replay of old events while ensuring no events are missed in subscription streams

definitions.definition: EventDataIB SHALL contain exactly one of: EpochTimestamp, SystemTimestamp, DeltaEpochTimestamp, or DeltaSystemTimestamp. When delta present, all other timestamp fields SHALL be omitted.
definitions.security_relevance: Enforcing mutual exclusion prevents timestamp ambiguity that could allow timing attack bypasses

definitions.definition: EpochTimestamp is UTC-based (microseconds since 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC). SystemTimestamp is monotonic system clock (microseconds since boot). Delta variants encode relative to prior event of same type.
definitions.security_relevance: Timestamp type distinction prevents confusion between wall-clock and monotonic time affecting replay detection and temporal ordering

definitions.definition: InvokeResponseIB SHALL contain exactly one of Command (CommandDataIB) or Status (CommandStatusIB). Never both, never neither. Command indicates success with response data, Status indicates error or success without data.
definitions.security_relevance: Enforcing mutual exclusion prevents response ambiguity that could cause incorrect command result processing

security_properties.description: Tag compression state SHALL NOT leak across interaction model Action boundaries
Wildcard paths SHALL be fully expanded to concrete paths before authorization checks to prevent coarse-grained ACL bypass
ListIndex SHALL only be omitted or null, any numeric value SHALL be rejected as error to prevent out-of-bounds access
List write errors leave list in uncertain state due to chunked processing, clients SHALL NOT assume rollback
Multi-command InvokeRequests SHALL have unique CommandRef for each command to enable unambiguous correlation
critical_transitions: CommandIdle -> ErrorState_CommandCorrelationFailure when refs not unique

security_properties.description: EventNumber SHALL monotonically increase within stream to detect replay or reordering attacks
When delta timestamp present, all other timestamp fields SHALL be omitted to prevent ambiguity
Context tags SHALL be emitted in specification-defined order to reduce parser complexity and prevent ambiguity
Reports SHALL contain either success data XOR error status, never both or neither, for unambiguous result indication
Node tag omission SHALL only occur when target matches server NodeID, preventing routing confusion
Group ID SHALL be encoded in message header DST field and elided from path to prevent group confusion
ACL and Extension attributes allow single-IB pattern in WriteRequest while other lists do not, to enable atomic ACL updates
DataVersion compression SHALL follow same Action boundary isolation as path compression to prevent version confusion
DataVersionFilterIB SHALL correctly match cluster paths and compare versions to suppress unchanged data
critical_transitions: DataVersionFilterMatching -> DataVersionFilterPassed/Failed based on version comparison

security_properties.description: EventFilterIB SHALL correctly compare EventNumber against EventMin threshold to suppress old events
critical_transitions: EventFilterMatching -> EventFilterPassed/Failed based on EventNumber >= EventMin

security_properties.description: EventDataIB SHALL contain exactly one timestamp type, enforcing mutual exclusion constraint
critical_transitions: TimestampTypeValidating -> ErrorState_TimestampValidationFailed when multiple or no timestamps present

security_properties.description: Epoch and System timestamps SHALL be distinguished and processed separately to prevent clock domain confusion
InvokeResponseIB SHALL contain exactly one of Command or Status, never both or neither
critical_transitions: InvokeResponseValidating -> ErrorState_InvokeResponseInvalid when both or neither present

security_assumptions.assumption: TLV parsers correctly validate structure schemas and reject malformed encodings
security_assumptions.if_violated: Type confusion, buffer overflows, injection attacks via malformed structures

security_assumptions.assumption: Action boundaries are correctly tracked and compression state does not leak across actions
security_assumptions.if_violated: Path confusion attacks where compressed paths resolve to unintended targets from prior actions

security_assumptions.assumption: Wildcard path expansion occurs before ACL checks on each concrete path
security_assumptions.if_violated: Privilege escalation where wildcard ACL check grants access to individually unauthorized resources

security_assumptions.assumption: List write errors result in uncertain state and clients re-read lists after errors
security_assumptions.if_violated: Clients assume rollback and proceed with stale state, missing partially-applied changes

security_assumptions.assumption: CommandRef values are unique within InvokeRequest for proper correlation
security_assumptions.if_violated: Response confusion where one command's result is misapplied to another command

security_assumptions.assumption: EventNumber is monotonically assigned per stream and checked by receivers
security_assumptions.if_violated: Replay attacks succeed as receivers cannot detect non-monotonic event numbers

security_assumptions.assumption: Delta timestamp reconstruction maintains last absolute timestamp correctly across events
security_assumptions.if_violated: Timestamp reconstruction errors break temporal ordering for time-based security

security_assumptions.assumption: Context tags are emitted in order and parsers enforce ordering
security_assumptions.if_violated: Parser confusion with out-of-order tags potentially enabling field misinterpretation

security_assumptions.assumption: Node omission semantics are consistently interpreted by sender and receiver
security_assumptions.if_violated: Routing confusion where messages are processed by unintended nodes

security_assumptions.assumption: Group ID in message header DST field matches group addressing intent
security_assumptions.if_violated: Group confusion where messages route to wrong fabric group

security_assumptions.assumption: ListIndex numeric values are rejected and only omitted/null are accepted
security_assumptions.if_violated: Undefined behavior with numeric indices enables out-of-bounds access or inconsistent list operations

security_assumptions.assumption: ACL and Extension attributes receive special handling for single-IB writes
security_assumptions.if_violated: ACL updates may fail or behave unpredictably using wrong patterns

security_assumptions.assumption: DataVersion compression inheritance follows same Action boundary rules as path compression
security_assumptions.if_violated: DataVersion confusion where compressed IBs inherit wrong version from prior actions

security_assumptions.assumption: DataVersionFilterIB correctly matches cluster paths and compares versions
security_assumptions.if_violated: Filter suppression errors causing missing data updates or unnecessary data transmission

security_assumptions.assumption: EventFilterIB EventMin thresholds are correctly compared against EventNumber
security_assumptions.if_violated: Event suppression errors causing replay of old events or missing recent events

security_assumptions.assumption: Exactly one timestamp type is present in EventDataIB (one-of constraint enforced)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Timestamp ambiguity or missing timestamps breaking temporal ordering

security_assumptions.assumption: Epoch and System timestamp types are correctly distinguished and processed separately
security_assumptions.if_violated: Timestamp type confusion causing incorrect time comparisons across different clock domains

security_assumptions.assumption: InvokeResponseIB contains exactly one of Command or Status (mutual exclusion enforced)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Response ambiguity where both or neither present causing processing errors
